channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: My First RAG System Had No Evals. 40% of Answers Were Wrong.
item.description: When I started building production RAG systems, I noticed something: nobody was measuring retrieval quality. Teams would ship a system, ask users if it "felt good," and move on. No metrics. No...

item.title: I Built an AI Trading Bot That Watches Trump's Truth Social Posts
item.description: It started the way these things always do. I was watching the market one afternoon and saw a stock move right after a Truth Social post. Not a subtle drift, either. A real move. And I thought:...

item.title: Your VS Code Extensions Are a Supply Chain Attack Surface
item.description: Last week a VS Code extension called specstudio.code-wakatime-activity-tracker was caught dropping a Zig-compiled binary onto developer machines. It looked like WakaTime. It tracked your activity...

item.title: Show Dev: Taxhance — AI-powered tax document collection software for CPA firms
item.description: TL;DR Taxhance is tax document collection software built for CPA firms. It replaces the manual back-and-forth of chasing clients for missing documents with a secure client portal, AI-powered...

item.title: How WebAssembly Powers Free Browser Tools With No Login
item.description: There's a reason Squoosh can compress your images using codecs that rival desktop apps — and it has nothing to do with server power. The compression happens entirely in your browser tab, using a...

item.title: Introducing Side-by-Side Store Compare: See How Any Two UCP Stores Stack Up
item.description: Three months into running UCPChecker, the most common follow-up question we get from anyone reading a status report is the same: "OK, but how does that compare to [other store]?" That question...

item.title: RAMageddon has come for Microsoft’s Surface Pro and Surface Laptop
item.description: Microsoft just raised the prices across its line of Surface devices amid the global RAM shortage. Now, the 13-inch Surface Pro 11 and the 13.8-inch Surface Laptop 7 cost $500 more than their...

item.title: We Stopped Writing Code First. Here's Why Our Projects Got Better.
item.description: There's a moment in every project where you realize you've been building the wrong thing. For us, it happened on a client project last year. Three weeks of clean code. Solid architecture. Tests...

item.title: How I Built a Multi-Agent Code Review Pipeline
item.description: How I Built a Multi-Agent Code Review Pipeline I was mass-approving PRs at 11pm on a Thursday. Not reading them. Just scrolling, checking if tests passed, hitting approve. We've all been there....

item.title: SSH hardening checklist for production servers
item.description: My Production SSH Configuration Was a Mess. Here's How I Fixed It. SSH used to be a simple tool for me. Set it up, maybe change the port, and that was it. It worked fine until I inherited a fleet...

item.title: Fixing Abstraction Leakage: Standardizing Error Handling Across Layered Services
item.description: Introduction In the intricate machinery of layered Go services, abstraction leakage emerges as a silent saboteur, eroding encapsulation and sowing chaos in error handling. Picture this: a database...

item.title: NZXT to pay $3.45 million settlement over Flex PC rentals
item.description: NZXT and its business partner Fragile have agreed to pay $3.45 million to settle a class-action lawsuit that accused the companies of attempting to "scam" consumers through their Flex PC rental...

item.title: AI influencers are ‘everywhere’ at Coachella
item.description: Coachella kicked off on Friday, and as usual, it's the place to be for online influencers looking to show off their memorable experiences at the festival. A quick scroll through my social media...

item.title: Microsoft is working on yet another OpenClaw-like agent
item.description: The new features would be geared toward enterprise customers, with better security controls than the famously risky open source OpenClaw agent.

item.title: Stanford report highlights growing disconnect between AI insiders and everyone else
item.description: Stanford’s latest AI Index shows a widening gap between experts and the public, with rising anxiety over jobs, healthcare, and the economy.

item.title: The Apple Watch Series 11 has returned to best-ever price
item.description: If you’re still holding onto an older Apple Watch, now might be a good time to upgrade. Right now, the 42mm Apple Watch Series 11 with GPS is on sale for around $299 ($100 off) at Amazon, Best...

item.title: Xbox Game Pass ‘has become too expensive,’ says Microsoft’s new gaming chief in leaked memo
item.description: Microsoft's new Xbox chief, Asha Sharma, has signaled that Xbox Game Pass pricing is about to change. In an internal memo to Xbox employees, obtained by The Verge, Sharma admits that "Game Pass...

item.title: JanelaRAT Malware Targets Latin American Banks with 14,739 Attacks in Brazil in 2025
item.description: Banks and financial institutions in Latin American countries like Brazil and Mexico have continued to be the target of a malware family called JanelaRAT. A modified version of BX RAT, JanelaRAT is...

item.title: You can make a multicolor MacBook Neo out of Apple’s spare parts
item.description: If you're having trouble deciding on a color for a new MacBook Neo, you might not have to choose just one. The folks over at MacWorld found that Apple doesn't appear to limit the colors of spare...

item.title: On Anthropic’s Mythos Preview and Project Glasswing
item.description: The cybersecurity industry is obsessing over Anthropic’s new model, Claude Mythos Preview, and its effects on cybersecurity. Anthropic said that it is not releasing it to the general public...

item.title: Read OpenAI’s latest internal memo about beating the competition — including Anthropic
item.description: OpenAI's chief revenue officer, Denise Dresser, sent a four-page memo to employees on Sunday about the company's strategic direction, emphasizing the need to lock in users and grow its enterprise...

item.title: Meta Is Warned That Facial Recognition Glasses Will Arm Sexual Predators
item.description: More than 70 organizations, including the ACLU, EPIC, and Fight for the Future, say the AI smart glasses feature would endanger abuse victims, immigrants, and LGBTQ+ people.

item.title: Why opinion on AI is so divided
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. In an industry that doesn’t stand still, Stanford’s AI...

item.title: Slate raises $650 million to make its budget electric truck
item.description: Slate Auto, the EV startup backed by Jeff Bezos, raised $650 million to fund its effort to build an affordable electric pickup truck expected to start in the mid-$20,000s. The company plans on...

item.title: Microsoft is testing OpenClaw-like AI bots for Copilot
item.description: Microsoft is looking into ways it can integrate OpenClaw-style features into its Copilot AI assistant, according to a report from The Information. The test reportedly comes as part of efforts to...

item.title: Google’s Pixel 10A is a good midrange phone that’s $50 off
item.description: If it’s time for you to upgrade your phone, it’s worth considering the Pixel 10A, Google’s latest midrange phone that has some (but not all) of the new software features of the pricier $599 Pixel...

item.title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch signals IPO readiness as AI agents fuel revenue surge
item.description: While many startups founded prior to the emergence of ChatGPT are struggling to position themselves for the AI era, Vercel, a 10-year-old dev tool and website hosting platform, is benefiting from...

item.title: FBI and Indonesian Police Dismantle W3LL Phishing Network Behind $20M Fraud Attempts
item.description: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), in partnership with the Indonesian National Police, has dismantled the infrastructure associated with a global phishing operation that leveraged an...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Fiber Optic Spying, Windows Rootkit, AI Vulnerability Hunting and More
item.description: Monday is back, and the weekend’s backlog of chaos is officially hitting the fan. We are tracking a critical zero-day that has been quietly living in your PDFs for months, plus some aggressive...

item.title: Want to understand the current state of AI? Check out these charts.
item.description: If you’re following AI news, you’re probably getting whiplash. AI is a gold rush. AI is a bubble. AI is taking your job. AI can’t even read a clock. The 2026 AI Index from Stanford University’s...
